---
title: Sample Size computation methods for survival endpoints
format: 
  html
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Méthode de Schoenfeld

Le calcul de la taille d'échantillon de Schoenfeld, comme les autres méthodes de log rank se fait sous les hypothèses de loi exponentielles et de proportional hazard qui ne sont pas complètement satisfaisantes dans certain cas mais complètement ancré dans l'analyse de survie en générale.

Prenons $\alpha$ l'erreur de type I et $\beta$ l'erreur de type II. Alors, en notant $e$ le nombre d'evènement nécessaire, la formule (simple) de Schoenfeld donne :

$$
e = 2\times\frac{2(z_{1-\alpha/2} + z_{1 - \beta})^2}{\log(\text{HR})}
$${#eq-schoenfeld}

Où $z_x$ est le [z-score](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_score) de $x$, égale au quantile $(1-x)$ de la distribution normale standard.

## Lakatos
Chaine de Markov, mouvement brownien.


rpact :
The formula of Kim & Tsiatis (Biometrics, 1990) is used to calculate the expected number of events
under the alternative (see also Lakatos & Lan, Statistics in Medicine, 1992). These formulas are
generalized to piecewise survival times and non-constant piecewise accrual over time.

gsDesign2 :
Computes fixed design sample size (given power) or power (given sample size) by:
fixed_design_ahr() - Average hazard ratio method.
fixed_design_fh() - Weighted logrank test with Fleming-Harrington weights (Farrington and Manning, 1990).
fixed_design_mb() - Weighted logrank test with Magirr-Burman weights.
fixed_design_lf() - Lachin-Foulkes method (Lachin and Foulkes, 1986).
fixed_design_maxcombo() - MaxCombo method.
fixed_design_rmst() - RMST method.
fixed_design_milestone() - Milestone method.
Additionally, fixed_design_rd() provides fixed design for binary endpoint with treatment effect measuring in risk difference.

rashnu :
lakatosSampleSize : Lakatos
two_prop_size: on sait pas 
oneSurvSampleSize :
Fleming TR, Harrington DP. (1991). Counting Processes and Survival Analysis. New York: Wiley, pp. 236–237, Example 6.3.1.

Andersen PK, Borgan O, Gill RD, Keiding N. (1993). Statistical Models Based on Counting Processes. New York: Springer-Verlag, pp. 176–287, Section IV.1–3.

Bie O, Borgan O, Liestol K. (1987). Confidence intervals and confidence bands for the cumulative hazard rate function and their small sample properties. Scandinavian Journal of Statistics, 14(3), 221–233.

Borgan O, Liestol K. (1990). A note on confidence intervals and bands for the survival function based on transformations. Scandinavian Journal of Statistics, 17(1), 35–41.

Nagashima K, Noma H, Sato Y, Gosho M. (2020). Sample size calculations for single-arm survival studies using transformations of the Kaplan–Meier estimator. Pharmaceutical Statistics. https://doi.org/10.1002/pst.2090 Available at: https://arxiv.org/abs/2012.03355

Binary :
The Fisher exact test : east, nQuery, bbssr
The Z-pooled exact unconditional test (Z-pool) : east, nquery, bbssr, rpact
The Z-unpooled exact unconditional test (Z-unpooled) : east, nquery

https://stattools.crab.org/Calculators/oneNonParametricSurvival.htm
